title: Cephaelis spathicalyx
intro: Cephaelis spathicalyx, commonly known as wild caffeine, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve fatigue, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis spathicalyx include caffeine, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cephaelis spathicalyx include tea infusions, capsules, and extracts, which are used to support cognitive function, boost energy, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cephaelis spathicalyx is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the madder family. The genus Cephaelis is part of the tribe Palicoureeae, which is a group of plants that are mostly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Within the family Rubiaceae, Cephaelis spathicalyx is also part of the subfamily Cinchonoideae. This classification helps us understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Cephaelis spathicalyx.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal uses.
